channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Musk donated millions to GOP causes as early as 2022: Reports
item.description: Billionaire Elon Musk donated millions of dollars to Republican causes as early as 2022, much earlier than previously known, according to media reports.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Suncor Energy derives business value through business AI and automation
item.description: Suncor is legally required to charge tax on intercompany transactions, including those with companies outside of its control. To comply with this obligation, Suncor has developed the Intercompany Tax Automation (ITC) solution using SAP Business AI & SAP Build Products.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Businesses are looking for more automation at work, but fear disruption
item.description: Companies and employees recognize the benefits of automation, but businesses are understandably cautious.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Windows 11 Insider Previews: What’s in the latest build?
item.description: The Windows 11 2023 Update has been released, but behind the scenes, Microsoft is constantly working to improve the newest version of Windows.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: L’economia digitale di fronte alla sfida della regolamentazione
item.description: Questo è forse uno dei dibattiti più frequenti quando si parla di legislazione in materia digitale: l’onere normativo ostacola l’innovazione? E, di conseguenza, fino a che punto è necessario regolamentare per creare un quadro solido, ma che faciliti le nuove proposte? L’eterno dilemma contrappone te
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Why CIOs should embrace the potential of data and analytics enablement platforms for a brighter future
item.description: Technology leaders want to harness the power of their data to gain intelligence about what their customers want and how they want it. This is why the overall data and analytics (D&A) market is projected to grow astoundingly and expected to jump to $279.3 billion by 2030.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Snapchat’s failure to protect kids revealed in New Mexico lawsuit
item.description: Snapchat failed to act on “rampant” reports of child grooming, sextortion and other dangers to minors on its platform, according to a newly unredacted complaint against the company filed by New Mexico’s attorney general. Attorney General Raúl Torrez filed the original complaint on Sept.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Apple accused of violating labor laws, again
item.description: Apple has been accused of violating union rights, according to a complaint filed by the US National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) .
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: ChatGPT maker OpenAI raises $6.6 billion in fresh funding as it moves away from its nonprofit roots
item.description: OpenAI says it has raised $6.6 billion in venture capital investments as part of a broader shift by the ChatGPT maker away from its nonprofit roots.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: California at the Forefront of AI Innovation and Regulation
item.description: AI safety regulations in California, Nvidia's tech ETF impact, AI-powered cameras in San Francisco, and Hollywood studio's integration of AI into filmmaking.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Spring AI: An AI framework for Java developers
item.description: Artificial intelligence has been something of a fiesta for programmers for the last few years, and one language—Python—has been the undeniable belle of the ball. Java and other languages have been a bit sidelined.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Push to map Great Lakes bottom gains momentum amid promises effort will help fishing and shipping
item.description: What lies beneath the Great Lakes' waves is largely unknown, but there's a new push to learn more about thousands of shipwrecks, underwater infrastructure and the impacts of climate change on the bottom of the world's largest freshwater system.
item.dc:creator: Todd Richmond, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Sonos outlines a plan to win back user trust after its app fiasco
item.description: Speaker company Sonos laid out a number of new plans and commitments on Tuesday in an attempt to turn around the beleaguered sound company following a bug-riddled app relaunch in May that’s still impacting the company.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: It’s a wrap for the HoloLens 2 headset
item.description: Microsoft on Tuesday confirmed it is no longer producing the HoloLens 2, the company’s second-generation augmented reality headset first launched five years ago at Mobile World Congress (MWC) in Barcelona.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The Black List founder says Hollywood is thinking about risk all wrong
item.description: After altering how Hollywood discovers scripts, The Black List is now targeting the book world. Founder and CEO Franklin Leonard proved that overlooked scripts like Slumdog Millionaire and I, Tonya can become commercial and artistic hits.
item.dc:creator: Robert Safian, Fast Company

item.title: Australia's online dating industry adopts code of conduct to keep users safer
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms, Australia’s government said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Details from New Mexico's lawsuit against Snap show site failed to act on reports of sextortion
item.description: Snapchat failed to act on “rampant” reports of child grooming, sextortion and other dangers to minors on its platform, according to a newly unredacted complaint against the company filed by New Mexico's attorney general. Attorney General Raúl Torrez filed the original complaint on Sept.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: X requests it be reinstated in Brazil after complying with judge's orders, sources say
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — In the high-stakes showdown between the world’s richest man and a Brazilian Supreme Court justice, Elon Musk blinked. Musk’s social media site X has complied with Alexandre de Moraes’ orders and requested its service be reestablished in the country, two sources said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Journalist booted from X after publishing hacked Vance document
item.description: Klippenstein published the 271-page report compiled by the Trump campaign to vet Vance on his Substack.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Rep. Mace questions GSA’s plan for replacing FedRAMP JAB
item.description: Rep. Nancy Mace (R-S.C.), chairwoman of the Oversight and Accountability Subcommittee on Cybersecurity, IT and Innovation, is asking for a briefing by Oct. 3. The post Rep. Mace questions GSA’s plan for replacing FedRAMP JAB first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network
